apply: boost the can/CTA saturation and edge contrast vs background
text: Pepsi cola ad: single hero can, deep blue field, white logo at high contrast; minimal background clutter keeps fixation on the product and tagline.

apply: add a soft warm highlight behind the product to lift it off the scene
text: Coca-Cola ad: red-dominant frame, condensation on a backlit bottle; warm rim light draws the eye to the bottle silhouette and the script logo.

apply: anchor the logo near the optical center, large and unobstructed
text: Dr Pepper ad: maroon palette, oversized centered logo, faces kept to the periphery so they don't steal gaze from the brand mark.

apply: clear visual clutter around the logo so it owns its space
text: Nike ad: stark negative space, single athlete, swoosh in the lower third with nothing competing; eye lands on athlete then swoosh.

apply: use diagonal composition lines that point toward the product/logo
text: Adidas ad: diagonal track lines guide the gaze from the model's stride down to the three-stripe mark.

apply: isolate the product on a clean seamless background, kill distractors
text: Apple ad: product floats on seamless gradient, no human faces, specular highlights define the device; attention is almost entirely on the product.

apply: make the screen content the brightest, highest-contrast region
text: Samsung ad: the lit display is the brightest patch in frame, pulling fixation to the screen and logo beneath.

apply: saturate the food/product so warm tones dominate the gaze
text: McDonald's ad: warm reds/yellows, the product hyper-saturated against a muted ground; the food is the focal point.

apply: add crisp product texture detail to reward and hold fixation
text: Burger King ad: macro flame-grill texture on the patty; high-frequency detail holds the eye on the product.

apply: duotone the scene so a single bright CTA wins attention
text: Spotify ad: bold duotone imagery, one vivid green CTA that is the only saturated element, so it owns the gaze.

apply: keep the can high-contrast and central; lean edgy minimalism
text: Liquid Death ad: stark black/white, the tallboy can centered and high-contrast; minimal scene so the can dominates.

apply: orient action/motion so it visually points at the can and logo
text: Red Bull ad: athlete mid-air, motion vector resolves toward the can; logo placed at the motion endpoint.
